Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Identify cases A case should represent a logical unit of work Looking at the log we see the user works on different documents and applications

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Possible cases Working on a Word document Document1 docx Working on an email in Google Chrome Email Inbox Working on a PDF Report Draft pdf Working on an Excel file Budget 2024 xlsx Working on another Word document Quarterly Report docx

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We can define a case by the document or email being worked on For the email we can consider the email thread as a case For PDF the PDF file For Excel the spreadsheet

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Looking at the log the email actions are Open Email about Annual Meeting Scroll Reply TYPING Send Email We can consider this as a case for the email thread about the Annual Meeting

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Given the log I propose the following case IDs Case1 Document1 docx Case2 Email AnnualMeeting Case3 Report Draft pdf Case4 Budget 2024 xlsx Case5 Quarterly Report docx

Name: Validation - PE
Text: However note that the user works on Document1 docx in two separate sessions We can merge them into one case Document1 docx because it is the same document

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Alternatively we can split by continuous work on a document But the log shows the user returns to Document1 docx after Excel So we can consider the case for Document1 docx as having two separate segments

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Rules For Microsoft Word the case ID is the window title the document name For Google Chrome with window title Email Inbox we use the email subject from the action when available For Adobe Acrobat the case ID is the window title For Microsoft Excel the case ID is the window title

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Since we are focusing on the case as the document email we can ignore the SWITCH events and only consider the events that are directly about the case like FOCUS TYPING SAVE and assign them to the case of the window they are in

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: For the email events we assign case ID Annual Meeting For the PDF events case ID Report Draft pdf For Excel case ID Budget 2024 xlsx Then when the user returns to Document1 docx we assign the events to Document1 docx And then for Quarterly Report docx we assign to Quarterly Report docx

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We need to map the raw actions to activity names FOCUS Open Document TYPING Edit Content SAVE Save Document CLOSE Close Document CLICK in email Open Email Reply to Email Send Email SCROLL Scroll Document HIGHLIGHT Highlight Text

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For email we have specific actions CLICK with Action Open Email maps to Open Email CLICK with Action Reply maps to Reply to Email CLICK with Action Send maps to Send Email TYPING in email maps to Compose Email SCROLL in email maps to Scroll Email

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: We missed that event We should include it as an event for Quarterly Report docx We can map it to Resume Document or Open Document Since we already have an Open Document at 08 59 50 we can map this FOCUS to Resume Document

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We grouped events by the document or email thread being worked on Each document or email thread is considered a case The case ID is derived from the window title for documents or the email subject for email We mapped the raw actions to higher level activity names that represent the users process steps We ignored SWITCH events because they are transitions between cases and do not represent activities within a case
